title: delphinium hybridum subsp. ochroleucum
intro: Delphinium hybridum subsp. ochroleucum, also known as the yellow-leafed larkspur or white larkspur, is a perennial flowering plant species within the delphinium genus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots and flowers, which contain delphinine, a toxic alkaloid with potential health benefits. The root of Delphinium hybridum subsp. ochroleucum has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Delphinium hybridum subsp. ochroleucum, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, have been used to harness its medicinal properties, but caution is advised due to the plant's toxicity.
